message:
fix(fcp-oauth): accept RFC 6749 §3.1.2 registered query (br-i58yx)

crates/fcp-oauth/src/redirect_allowlist.rs previously rejected every
pre-registered redirect_uri that carried a query component, and
normalize_callback_redirect_uri stripped the entire query before
allowlist comparison. RFC 6749 §3.1.2 explicitly permits a registered
query component on the redirection endpoint — a standards-compliant
tenant-aware client configured with
`https://client.example/callback?tenant=acme` could not pass
OAuth2Client::new(), and even the callback allowlist helpers had no
way to represent that registration.

## Fix

1. normalize_registered_redirect_uri now shape-checks with
   `allow_query = true`. A registered redirect MAY carry query pairs.

2. A new collision gate rejects registered query pairs whose keys
   match the standard OAuth response parameter names (code, state,
   error, error_description, error_uri, iss). The provider appends
   those names to the callback; if the registered URI pre-reserved
   one, the callback split would be ambiguous.

3. normalize_callback_redirect_uri now STRIPS only the OAuth response
   parameters via a new strip_oauth_response_params helper, preserving
   any registered query pairs in the result. Allowlist equality then
   still matches against the registered entry.

Round-trip example:
  registered:  https://client.example/callback?tenant=acme
  callback:    https://client.example/callback?tenant=acme&code=X&state=Y
  normalised:  https://client.example/callback?tenant=acme  (matches)

## Regression tests (5 new unit + 1 new + 1 replaced in oauth2.rs)

  normalize_registered_accepts_rfc6749_query_component
  normalize_registered_rejects_oauth_response_param_in_query (all 6 names)
  normalize_callback_preserves_registered_query_alongside_oauth_response_params
  callback_allowlisted_when_registered_query_component_matches
  callback_rejected_when_registered_query_component_mismatches

  oauth2::tests::test_client_rejects_redirect_uri_with_query  (removed)
  oauth2::tests::test_client_accepts_redirect_uri_with_registered_query_component (new)
  oauth2::tests::test_client_rejects_redirect_uri_with_oauth_response_param_in_query (new)

## Golden snapshot

Regenerated crates/fcp-oauth/tests/snapshots/golden_snapshots__
redirect_allowlist_decisions.snap with new / renamed entries:
  registered_query_not_in_allowlist                   — query accepted,
                                                        fails membership
  registered_query_collides_with_response_param       — collision gate
  callback_extra_query_not_in_registered_allowlist    — extra query OK
                                                        through shape;
                                                        fails allowlist
  callback_registered_query_preserved_ok              — positive path

## Verified

  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-swarm-p2 cargo test --offline -p fcp-oauth
  -> 616 + 15 + 59 passing across lib / golden / no-mock; 0 failed.

Closes flywheel_connectors-i58yx.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-i32c.3","title":"TEST: fcp.mailchimp Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)","description":"## Goal
Mock-only unit/integration tests for this connector.

## Required coverage
- schema validation (inputs + outputs)
- error taxonomy mapping (retry semantics)
- pagination and bounds
- rate limit behavior
- redaction (no token/secret/PII leakage)
- idempotency + receipts for Dangerous operations

## Logging
All tests emit structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic tests; no live external calls.
- Includes adversarial cases (timeouts, malformed responses, partial failures).

Run the shared connector compliance runner and capture an evidence bundle.

## Must validate
- handshake + introspection correctness
- default deny (no CapabilityToken → deny + DecisionReceipt)
- NetworkConstraints enforcement
- receipts/audit for Dangerous operations
- structured logs

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E run is deterministic and produces a stable artifact bundle.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-i4zm","title":"Revocation System (RevocationObject/Registry/Chain)","description":"## Overview

Implement the revocation system as specified in V2 spec §14.3. This is CRITICAL security infrastructure - the spec explicitly states: "Without revocation, 'compromised device' recovery is mostly imaginary."

Revocations are mesh objects distributed like any other object and MUST be enforced before use.

## Background & Justification

When a device is compromised or a key is leaked, the mesh MUST be able to:
1. Revoke capabilities issued to that device
2. Revoke the device's issuer key (can no longer mint tokens)
3. Remove the device from the mesh entirely
4. Revoke compromised connector binaries
5. Force zone key rotation

Without revocation, security incidents cannot be recovered from.

## Normative Types (V2 Spec §14.3)

### RevocationObject
```rust
pub struct RevocationObject {
    pub header: ObjectHeader,
    /// ObjectIds being revoked
    pub revoked: Vec<ObjectId>,
    /// Scope of revocation
    pub scope: RevocationScope,
    /// Human-readable reason
    pub reason: String,
    /// When revocation becomes effective
    pub effective_at: u64,
    /// Optional expiry (None = permanent)
    pub expires_at: Option<u64>,
    /// Owner signature (revocations MUST be owner-signed)
    pub signature: Signature,
}
```

### RevocationScope
```rust
pub enum RevocationScope {
    /// Revoke capability objects/tokens
    Capability,
    /// Revoke issuer keys (node can no longer mint tokens)
    IssuerKey,
    /// Revoke node attestation (removes device from mesh)
    NodeAttestation,
    /// Revoke zone key (forces rotation)
    ZoneKey,
    /// Revoke connector binary (supply chain response)
    ConnectorBinary,
}
```

### RevocationEvent (Chain Node)
```rust
/// Hash-linked chain for revocation freshness
pub struct RevocationEvent {
    pub header: ObjectHeader,
    pub revocation_object_id: ObjectId,
    pub prev: Option<ObjectId>,
    /// Monotonic chain sequence number (NORMATIVE)
    /// Enables O(1) freshness comparison: seq_a > seq_b ⟹ a is fresher than b
    pub seq: u64,
    pub occurred_at: u64,
    pub signature: Signature,
}
```

### RevocationHead (Checkpoint)
```rust
/// Enables freshness semantics: tokens bound to a rev_head, verifiers MUST
/// have revocation state >= that head before accepting the token
pub struct RevocationHead {
    pub header: ObjectHeader,
    pub zone_id: ZoneId,
    pub head_event: ObjectId,
    /// Sequence number of head_event (NORMATIVE)
    /// Enables O(1) freshness comparison without chain traversal
    pub head_seq: u64,
    pub epoch_id: EpochId,
    pub quorum_signatures: Vec<(TailscaleNodeId, Signature)>,
}
```

### RevocationRegistry
```rust
pub struct RevocationRegistry {
    revocations: HashMap<ObjectId, RevocationObject>,
    bloom_filter: BloomFilter,  // Fast negative lookup
    /// Latest revocation head known for this zone
    pub head: Option<ObjectId>,
}

impl RevocationRegistry {
    /// Check if object is revoked (MUST be called before use)
    pub fn is_revoked(&self, object_id: &ObjectId) -> bool {
        // Fast path: bloom filter definitely-not check
        if !self.bloom_filter.might_contain(object_id.as_bytes()) {
            return false;
        }
        self.revocations.contains_key(object_id)
    }
}
```

## Implementation Requirements

### 1. RevocationObject Creation and Signing
- Owner-only signing (revocations are critical operations)
- Support all RevocationScope variants
- Validate effective_at is in future or now
- Propagate via mesh gossip immediately

### 2. RevocationEvent Chain
- Hash-linked for integrity
- Monotonic seq for O(1) freshness comparison
- Each new revocation creates new chain node
- Support chain traversal for full audit

### 3. RevocationHead Quorum
- Requires quorum signatures from mesh nodes
- Binds capability tokens to revocation state
- Verifiers MUST check token.rev_head <= local.head
- Enables freshness guarantees

### 4. Bloom Filter Optimization
- Fast negative lookup for non-revoked objects
- Size tuned for expected revocation volume
- Rebuild on restart from persistent store

### 5. Revocation Enforcement Points

EVERY capability verification MUST include:
```rust
fn verify_capability_token(&self, token: &CapabilityToken) -> Result<()> {
    // MANDATORY: Check revocation before any other validation
    if self.revocation_registry.is_revoked(&token.object_id) {
        return Err(Error::Revoked);
    }
    
    // Check revocation freshness
    if let Some(token_rev_head) = token.rev_head {
        if !self.revocation_registry.is_at_least(token_rev_head)? {
            return Err(Error::RevocationStateStale);
        }
    }
    
    // Continue with normal verification...
}
```

### 6. Revocation Propagation
- High-priority gossip (revocations supersede other traffic)
- Persist to local store immediately
- Trigger re-evaluation of cached capability decisions

## Use Cases by Scope

### Capability Revocation
- Revoke specific capability tokens (e.g., leaked token)
- Immediate effect on all nodes

### IssuerKey Revocation  
- Node can no longer mint new tokens
- Existing tokens remain valid until they expire
- Used for semi-trusted devices

### NodeAttestation Revocation
- Complete device removal from mesh
- All capabilities issued TO and BY this node revoked
- Used for compromised devices

### ZoneKey Revocation
- Forces zone key rotation
- All zone members must re-enroll
- Used for catastrophic key compromise

### ConnectorBinary Revocation
- Specific binary hash revoked
- Connectors using that binary MUST stop
- Used for supply chain incidents

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] RevocationObject with all scope variants
- [ ] Owner-only signature validation
- [ ] RevocationEvent hash-linked chain
- [ ] Monotonic seq numbers for O(1) freshness
- [ ] RevocationHead with quorum signatures
- [ ] RevocationRegistry with bloom filter
- [ ] is_revoked() check at all enforcement points
- [ ] Revocation freshness comparison
- [ ] High-priority gossip propagation
- [ ] Persistent storage with crash recovery
- [ ] Audit events for all revocation operations

## Dependencies

- Core Types (ObjectHeader, ObjectId, etc.)
- Zone Architecture (zone-scoped revocations)
- Capability System (enforcement points)
- MeshNode (gossip propagation)
- Observability (audit events)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T09:44:25.848323653Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T09:47:20.691101782Z","closed_at":"2026-01-15T09:47:20.691101782Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of 1n78.9 (enhanced with detailed V2 spec content)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-i4zm","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.17","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-i4zm","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.28","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-i4zm","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-i4zm","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-i58yx","title":"[REVIEW-P2][MEDIUM] fcp-oauth redirect_uri hardening rejects RFC-valid registered query components","description":"crates/fcp-oauth/src/oauth2.rs:321-327 now routes every configured redirect_uri through normalize_registered_redirect_uri(), and crates/fcp-oauth/src/redirect_allowlist.rs:116-145 rejects any registered redirect URI with a query string. OAuth 2.0 allows pre-registered query components on the redirection endpoint (RFC 6749 section 3.1.2), and providers commonly preserve them while appending code/state. Bug scenario: a standards-compliant client configured with https://client.example/callback?tenant=acme now fails OAuth2Client::new(), and even the callback allowlist helpers cannot represent such redirects because normalize_callback_redirect_uri() strips the entire query before comparison. Suggested fix: allow registered redirects to carry a fixed query component, preserve that component during normalization, and strip only the provider-added OAuth response parameters when validating callbacks.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:15:19Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:45:01.858190Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-i58yx","title":"[REVIEW-P2][MEDIUM] fcp-oauth redirect_uri hardening rejects RFC-valid registered query components","description":"crates/fcp-oauth/src/oauth2.rs:321-327 now routes every configured redirect_uri through normalize_registered_redirect_uri(), and crates/fcp-oauth/src/redirect_allowlist.rs:116-145 rejects any registered redirect URI with a query string. OAuth 2.0 allows pre-registered query components on the redirection endpoint (RFC 6749 section 3.1.2), and providers commonly preserve them while appending code/state. Bug scenario: a standards-compliant client configured with https://client.example/callback?tenant=acme now fails OAuth2Client::new(), and even the callback allowlist helpers cannot represent such redirects because normalize_callback_redirect_uri() strips the entire query before comparison. Suggested fix: allow registered redirects to carry a fixed query component, preserve that component during normalization, and strip only the provider-added OAuth response parameters when validating callbacks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:15:19Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:49:19.471471Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:49:19.470491Z","close_reason":"Relaxed normalize_registered_redirect_uri to accept pre-registered query components per RFC 6749 §3.1.2 (was rejecting any query outright). Added a new collision gate: a registered query key that matches an OAuth response parameter name (code/state/error/error_description/error_uri/iss) is rejected because the callback split would be ambiguous. normalize_callback_redirect_uri now STRIPS only the OAuth response parameters via a new strip_oauth_response_params helper, preserving any registered query pairs so allowlist equality works. A standards-compliant tenant-aware URL https://client.example/callback?tenant=acme now round-trips: it validates at config time, and a callback carrying tenant=acme&code=X&state=Y normalizes to https://client.example/callback?tenant=acme which matches the registered entry. Added 5 new regression tests (normalize_registered_accepts_rfc6749_query_component, normalize_registered_rejects_oauth_response_param_in_query, normalize_callback_preserves_registered_query_alongside_oauth_response_params, callback_allowlisted_when_registered_query_component_matches, callback_rejected_when_registered_query_component_mismatches) + updated test_client_rejects_redirect_uri_with_query to its successor test_client_accepts_redirect_uri_with_registered_query_component + added test_client_rejects_redirect_uri_with_oauth_response_param_in_query. Regenerated the golden snapshot with new labels (registered_query_not_in_allowlist, registered_query_collides_with_response_param, callback_extra_query_not_in_registered_allowlist, callback_registered_query_preserved_ok). Verified: 616 lib + 15 golden + 59 no-mock tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-i5iv4","title":"[REVIEW-P2][HIGH] fcp-registry TUF/Sigstore gate still trusts caller-forgeable verification flags","description":"crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:304-314 makes SupplyChainEvidence a public struct with public tuf_verified/sigstore_verified booleans, crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:932-937 accepts Option<&SupplyChainEvidence> directly from the caller, and crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:1724-1739 now treats those booleans as the entire oracle for require_tuf/require_sigstore. Bug scenario: any embedding code can instantiate SupplyChainEvidence { tuf_verified: true, sigstore_verified: true, ..Default::default() } without running a verifier adapter and still satisfy the supposedly real verification gate, so c046efde replaces a presence-only bypass with a caller-asserted-bool bypass. Suggested fix: make the verified fields private and construct SupplyChainEvidence only from verifier result types (or perform TUF/Sigstore verification inside RegistryVerifier instead of trusting caller-supplied booleans).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:15:19Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:31:56.079149Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:31:56.078898Z","close_reason":"Made SupplyChainEvidence::tuf_verified and sigstore_verified fields PRIVATE. Added constructor-only promotion paths: with_tuf_verification_result(&TufVerificationResult) and with_sigstore_verification_result(&SigstoreVerificationResult) that only flip the flag when result.verified == true. Added pub getters tuf_verified() / sigstore_verified(). Test-only escape hatch mark_tuf_verified_for_tests() / mark_sigstore_verified_for_tests() gated behind #[cfg(any(test, feature = 'test-mocks'))] so downstream release builds cannot reach them. Migrated all 2 production call sites (fwc/supply_chain_cmd.rs, fwc/main.rs) + 4 integration test sites (fcp-registry/tests/no_mock_integration.rs) + 2 internal pcmm8 tests to the new API. Added DenyReason::IpNotAllowed handling to fwc/net_cmd.rs rule_id_for + suggestion_for arms. 2 new regression tests supply_chain_evidence_refuses_failed_tuf_verification_result + supply_chain_evidence_refuses_failed_sigstore_verification_result confirm failed verification results do NOT promote the flag. Verified: 264 lib tests pass (was 262 + 2 new), 88 integration tests pass, 563 fcp-sandbox lib tests pass. Pre-existing base_manifest_toml fixture-drift failure (98 tests) is unrelated and reproduces on HEAD without these changes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-i7nk","title":"TEST: Error Taxonomy Unit Tests (FCP-XXXX Codes, Retry Semantics, AI Hints)","description":"# TEST: Error Taxonomy Unit Tests


## Goal
Validate the error taxonomy is stable, code-complete, and machine-actionable (retry semantics + hints) with golden vectors.

## Scope
Comprehensive tests for error code system and recovery hints.

## Test Categories

### Error Code Ranges
- [ ] FCP-1xxx (protocol errors) mapped correctly
- [ ] FCP-2xxx (auth/capability errors) mapped correctly
- [ ] FCP-3xxx (zone errors) mapped correctly
- [ ] FCP-4xxx (store/mesh errors) mapped correctly
- [ ] FCP-5xxx (connector errors) mapped correctly
- [ ] FCP-9xxx (internal errors) mapped correctly

### Error Structure
- [ ] All errors have stable `code` string
- [ ] All errors have human-readable `message`
- [ ] All errors have `retryable` flag
- [ ] Retryable errors have `retry_after_ms`
- [ ] Errors include `details` when applicable

### AI Recovery Hints
- [ ] Top 20 failure modes have ai_recovery_hint
- [ ] Hints are actionable and specific
- [ ] Hints don't leak secrets
- [ ] Hints are stable across versions

### Error Serialization
- [ ] Errors serialize to canonical CBOR
- [ ] Errors deserialize correctly
- [ ] Unknown error codes handled gracefully
- [ ] Forward compatibility for new codes

### Retry Semantics
- [ ] Retryable errors retry correctly
- [ ] Non-retryable errors fail immediately
- [ ] Retry budget is respected
- [ ] Exponential backoff applied

## Golden Vectors
Location: `tests/vectors/errors/`
- `error_protocol.cbor`
- `error_auth.cbor`
- `error_zone.cbor`
- `error_retryable.cbor`

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "test_name": "test_error_taxonomy",
  "phase": "execute",
  "correlation_id": "uuid",
  "error_code": "FCP-2101",
  "retryable": false,
  "ai_hint_present": true,
  "result": "pass"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All error codes are tested
- AI hints exist for common failures
- Error serialization is stable

Format: for each claim in prior report, record current status, evidence, and delta. Publish in docs/quarterly/. This normalizes incompleteness and prevents bi-annual shock when claims are found to be stale. WHY: L2 Debiasing recommended institutional debiasing practices. Multi-agent development amplifies completion bias (agents optimize for task completion metrics, not honest assessment). Without periodic reality checks, documentation drift compounds over time. WHERE: Create docs/quarterly/ directory. Create template. First report covers the MOR analysis findings reconciliation. ACCEPTANCE: (1) Template exists. (2) First quarterly report produced. (3) Process documented in AGENTS.md or CONTRIBUTING.md. ESTIMATED EFFORT: 1 day to set up template; 1 day per quarterly report. DEPENDS ON: C2.4 (claims audit) — first quarterly report is the claims audit output.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:34:18Z"},{"id":2669,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-icndt","author":"jemanuel","text":"C2.5 complete.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-nvt","title":"fcp.sentry: Issue assignment and status management","description":"# fcp.sentry: Issue assignment and status management (FCP2)

## Goal
Allow safe, auditable manipulation of Sentry issue workflow state:
- assign/unassign
- resolve/unresolve
- ignore/unignore
- set priority (if supported)

These are write operations that can affect on-call workflows.

## Scope
### Write operations
- `sentry.issue.assign`
- `sentry.issue.resolve`
- `sentry.issue.ignore`
- `sentry.issue.update_priority`

### Read support
- fetch minimal issue metadata required for optimistic concurrency (if available).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `sentry.issues.write`

Policy guidance:
- Some orgs may require approvals for resolving/ignoring issues.

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- For every write:
  - emit OperationReceipt + AuditEvent
  - include issue id + action + correlation id
  - never include raw issue payloads in logs

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `sentry.issue.assign`
  - `sentry.issue.set_status` (resolve/ignore/unresolve)
  - `sentry.issue.set_priority`
- Denial reason codes for policy engine.

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - capability gating
  - approval gating (when policy requires)
  - error mapping (permission denied, conflict)
  - audit/receipt emission

## Acceptance criteria
- Issue workflow actions are safe, explainable, and auditable.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-nw3xn","title":"[FCP3/P4] Converge host and SDK on one execution model","description":"Background: once kernel, policy, evidence, and durable semantics exist as real owners, fcp-host and fcp-sdk must converge on one execution model. The host should orchestrate and supervise; the SDK should express the same lifecycle and semantics for connector authors; neither should redefine the platform.

Scope: refactor host into a consumer of those lower layers, publish a neutral SDK app contract, and adapt at least one connector family end to end. The phase must cover lifecycle, checkpoint, drain, health, resume, and error semantics with crate local unit tests, cross crate integration coverage, connector author ergonomics checks, and scripted end to end transcripts that include detailed structured diagnostics for both operator and connector author workflows. This phase is complete when host and SDK describe the same runtime, checkpoint, drain, health, and error semantics rather than parallel interpretations.

Acceptance: connector authors and host operators can rely on one execution vocabulary, one set of diagnostics, and reproducible test and transcript artifacts that prove the shared model in practice rather than only in architecture prose.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:01.782511Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-01T19:02:53.482981Z","closed_at":"2026-04-01T19:02:53.482824Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","host","mesh-first","phase-4","sdk"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-nw3xn","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-0jm9a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:13.291708Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-nw3xn","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1fu5d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:13.091610Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-nw3xn","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-6t9s0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:13.495939Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-nw3xn","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-ciy20","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:13.706332Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-nw3xn","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-tbjdk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:12.898042Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2051,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-nw3xn","author":"CobaltHarbor","text":"Host convergence is the point where the platform stops being conceptually centered on fcp-host. The host remains important, but as a node local root app over shared semantics rather than the place where platform truth is invented.","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:01Z"}]}
-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-o1na6","title":"[PERF] fcp-bootstrap::open_authenticated_session: global PKCS#11 lock serializes all sessions","description":"crates/fcp-bootstrap/src/hardware_token.rs:451-503 and 585-597. open_authenticated_session() grabs provider_probe_lock() before PKCS#11 initialize/open/login and keeps that MutexGuard alive inside the session close closure until the caller eventually closes the authenticated session. Enumeration sessions do the same for their whole lifetime. That turns hardware-token rotation, cert enumeration, and key enumeration into a process-wide serialized critical section: one long-lived session blocks unrelated providers/slots from even starting, so throughput collapses under parallel bootstrap or rotation work. Proposed improvement: narrow the lock to the init/finalize transition or make it provider-scoped/refcounted instead of holding one global mutex for the full lifetime of every authenticated or enumeration session.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:56:28.975339Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:42:14.916261Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-o1na6","title":"[PERF] fcp-bootstrap::open_authenticated_session: global PKCS#11 lock serializes all sessions","description":"crates/fcp-bootstrap/src/hardware_token.rs:451-503 and 585-597. open_authenticated_session() grabs provider_probe_lock() before PKCS#11 initialize/open/login and keeps that MutexGuard alive inside the session close closure until the caller eventually closes the authenticated session. Enumeration sessions do the same for their whole lifetime. That turns hardware-token rotation, cert enumeration, and key enumeration into a process-wide serialized critical section: one long-lived session blocks unrelated providers/slots from even starting, so throughput collapses under parallel bootstrap or rotation work. Proposed improvement: narrow the lock to the init/finalize transition or make it provider-scoped/refcounted instead of holding one global mutex for the full lifetime of every authenticated or enumeration session.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:56:28.975339Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:46:18.096552Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:46:18.096214Z","close_reason":"PKCS#11 session opens now use provider-scoped active-session accounting so init/finalize stay serialized without holding the global lock for the full authenticated session lifetime","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-o8kjw","title":"[verification] true interactive host-backed Mac E2E validation for fwc + Apple connectors","description":"Execute the first real, closed-loop interactive verification pass on this Mac for the current operator path `fwc -> fcp-host -> connector subprocesses` using fresh repo-built binaries.

Scope:
- Wave 0: fresh `fwc` + `fcp-host` live host-backed CLI sanity checks
- Wave 1: Apple Notes and Apple Reminders true E2E scenarios with out-of-band verification and evidence capture
- Maintain a granular TODO artifact so sub-steps, prompts, evidence, and cleanup are not lost

Acceptance criteria:
1. Fresh build/verification path is documented and executed, with `rch` used where feasible and any local-Mac build necessity explained.
2. A local `fcp-host` instance runs with a minimal connector inventory including Apple Notes and Apple Reminders.
3. At least one real Apple Notes write/read scenario and one real Apple Reminders create/complete scenario are executed through the live host-backed path.
4. Each scenario has evidence artifacts (commands, `fwc` output, host logs, out-of-band verification, screenshots if possible, cleanup note).
5. Remaining blockers for cloud/API follow-on targets are recorded truthfully.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-25T22:55:14.054205Z","created_by":"VioletSummit","updated_at":"2026-03-25T23:31:58.498323Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T23:31:58.498165Z","close_reason":"Completed live Apple verification wave; follow-up connector bugs tracked separately in flywheel_connectors-i05fu","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","mac","verification"],"comments":[{"id":2013,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-o8kjw","author":"VioletSummit","text":"Completed the live first-wave Mac verification scope.

What worked:
- Fresh repo-built `fwc` and `fcp-host` were exercised against a live local host on `127.0.0.1:8787`.
- Apple Notes: health, create, search, get, direct AppleScript read-back, and screenshot artifact all captured.
- Apple Reminders: health, create, complete, direct AppleScript read-back, and screenshot artifact all captured.

Important implementation outcome:
- Found and fixed a real host bug during this verification: subprocess connectors were configured but never handshaken before invoke/simulate. Patched `fcp-host` and verified the fix with the focused host test `subprocess_connector_invoke_performs_handshake_automatically`.

Live bugs uncovered:
- Filed follow-up `flywheel_connectors-i05fu` for Apple Reminders list/read round-trip failures seen on this Mac.

Evidence root:
- `artifacts/true-e2e/2026-03-25/`
- Detailed run log and checklist: `TODO_TRUE_INTERACTIVE_E2E_MAC_EXECUTION.md`","created_at":"2026-03-25T23:31:53Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-o8umn","title":"[FCP3/P6.3] Align doctor, preflight, show, health, and explain with durable evidence bundles","description":"Background: commands like doctor, preflight, show, health, and explain should ultimately be grounded in durable evidence bundles and canonical control plane semantics, not in scattered host local heuristics.

Scope: align these operator flows with the new evidence and durable plane model so they can explain not just current availability, but why the system believes a claim, what evidence backs it, which bundle or correlation handle to inspect next, and whether the claim is offline, host local, mesh backed, degraded, or fallback derived. Require operator friendly remediation hints, concise summary paths, deep drill down paths, and detailed structured diagnostic logs so failures can be investigated without code archaeology.

Acceptance: operator diagnostics and explanations use a durable and evidence backed model rather than ad hoc host local state scraping, and they materially improve a user's ability to understand and act on system state.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:08.359361Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","updated_at":"2026-04-06T04:06:25.759003Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T04:06:25.758786Z","close_reason":"All 3 children closed: o8umn.1 (evidence-lookup model), o8umn.2 (remediation-aware outputs), o8umn.3 (snapshot proof). 78 truth tests cover full operator evidence pipeline.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["evidence","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","logging","mesh-first","operator","phase-6"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-o8umn","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-0qtvu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:50.284483Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-o8umn","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-gg5sl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:49.581222Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-o8umn","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:32:48.991786Z","created_by":"CobaltHarbor","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-o8umn.1","title":"[FCP3/P6.3] Define the shared evidence-lookup model for operator commands","description":"Background: commands like doctor, preflight, show, health, and explain cannot be evidence-backed until they share one durable lookup model for bundles, traces, and correlation handles. Right now that relationship is still too implicit.

Scope: define the evidence-bundle lookup contract for the major operator commands. Cover how commands discover bundle IDs, correlation handles, trace references, and next-step drill-down links, and keep the model consistent enough that docs and support flows can teach it once.

Acceptance: operator commands use a common evidence lookup model, and later remediation and transcript beads can rely on that model instead of inventing command-specific bundle semantics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:54:55.572770Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","updated_at":"2026-04-06T03:45:54.132218Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T03:45:54.132001Z","close_reason":"Implemented: OperatorCommand, DrillDownAction, OperatorEvidenceLookup with from_resolution(), drill_down_hint(), to_structured_log(). Consistent model across all 6 operator commands. 66 truth tests pass (7 new).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["evidence","fcp3","fcp3-refoundation","operator","phase-6"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-o8umn.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-hoogu.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:56:43.963417Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-o8umn.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-kpj16.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:56:43.482890Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-o8umn.1","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-o8umn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-03T17:54:55.572770Z","created_by":"SunnyOak","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2416,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-o8umn.1","author":"SunnyOak","text":"Group operator-evidence note: every user-facing command in this track needs local semantic tests, host-backed integration coverage, and transcript bundles with remediation-aware structured logs. Preserve artifact handles and rerun commands so the deployment runbook, troubleshooting docs, and final proof bundle can cite this track directly.","created_at":"2026-04-03T20:12:13Z"},{"id":2499,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-o8umn.1","author":"jemanuel","text":"SunnyMoose 2026-04-05: Defined the shared evidence-lookup model for operator commands. New types: OperatorCommand (Doctor, Preflight, Show, Health, Explain, Status), DrillDownAction (label, command, rationale), OperatorEvidenceLookup (command, connector_id, knowledge_state, correlation_id, evidence_handles, drill_down, retrieval_command). Key methods: from_resolution() bridges TruthResolution to operator output, drill_down_hint() generates per-command trace commands, to_structured_log() serializes consistent JSON for all commands. 7 new tests covering: drill-down hints, lookup construction, drill-down actions, structured log shape, all-commands coverage, cross-command consistency, serde roundtrip. 66 total truth tests pass. rch verification green.","created_at":"2026-04-06T03:45:48Z"}]}
